authoritative_sources.title: Shortening the Securities Transaction Settlement Cycle
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC final rulemaking is the primary U.S. authority for T+1 settlement, same-day allocation and affirmation controls, and investment-adviser recordkeeping updates that directly govern post-trade workflow timing and evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Allocation, confirmation, affirmation, and recordkeeping control design
used_for: recordkeeping updates tied to investment advisers
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains when the lane may recommend release, what evidence must exist on trade date, and when missing timestamps or allocations block progression.

authoritative_sources.title: Risk Alert: Division of Examinations T+1 Settlement Cycle
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC examinations guidance is authoritative for operational readiness, testing, books-and-records support, and control weaknesses tied to allocations, confirmations, affirmations, and shortened settlement timing.
used_for: control failures that elevate settlement risk
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines what quality-review evidence should support a release, hold, or investigation recommendation and why incomplete readiness evidence blocks confident progression.

authoritative_sources.title: T+1 Settlement Cycle Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC FAQs are authoritative public guidance for transaction-scope boundaries, including when T+1 assumptions do not apply uniformly across products, markets, or security types.
used_for: cases that need specialized downstream handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane from overgeneralizing the industry name to every trade-like workflow and supports refusal or routing when exempt products or jurisdictions change the rule set.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 4511 - General Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 4511 is the books-and-records baseline for preserving and retrieving broker-dealer records that support trade-operations evidence, auditability, and completion discipline.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires the lane to tie outputs to retrievable system-of-record artifacts instead of relying on spreadsheets, email, or narrative assurance alone.

authoritative_sources.title: CTM Trade Affirm Factsheet
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC operates core institutional trade-processing infrastructure and is authoritative for public descriptions of central matching, affirmation workflow states, and same-day affirmation operating semantics.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines realistic trade-ops terms and decision points around matching, affirmation, and late affirm workflows so the lane stays tied to actual post-trade records.

authoritative_sources.title: ALERT Overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC ALERT is a market-infrastructure reference for standing settlement instruction golden-source handling, enrichment, and the SSI record set used to reduce instruction breaks and failed trades.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains settlement-readiness decisions so a matched trade is not treated as releasable when SSI evidence is stale, missing, or contradictory.

authoritative_sources.title: Standing Settlement Instruction Market Practice
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISITC is a recognized institutional trade-operations market-practice body whose SSI guidance directly defines the fields, ownership boundaries, and evidence discipline needed for safe manual and automated instruction handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SSI control evidence and manual-change discipline
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports refusal of unsupported manual SSI overrides and shapes the nonconformance evidence required when SSI records conflict or are incomplete.

authoritative_sources.title: ISITC Market Practices
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISITC market-practice resources are a recognized public reference for institutional post-trade workflow terms, matching expectations, and exception-safe operating behavior used across asset managers, custodians, and brokers.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Helps keep the lane specific to named post-trade workflows and avoids collapsing into generic finance or operations abstractions.

workflow_stages.name: Trade intake and prerequisite capture
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the trade is within supported product and market scope, identify the investment manager, executing broker, custodian, settlement location, and tenant operating model, and retrieve the authoritative order and execution records before any control disposition is suggested.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Allocation, matching, and affirmation control review
workflow_stages.description: Validate allocation completeness, broker-confirm alignment, matched versus unmatched status, and affirmation timing so the lane can distinguish work that is genuinely trade-date ready from work that only looks complete in a local tracker.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: SSI and settlement-readiness verification
workflow_stages.description: Check that standing settlement instructions, custodian-account details, settlement location, and tenant market-specific cut-off rules support a safe release recommendation instead of assuming that a matched trade is automatically ready.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Break, fail, and nonconformance handling
workflow_stages.description: Review DK, RAD, unmatched, fail-aging, and manual-intervention exceptions, identify whether they are isolated or recurring, and hold progression until authoritative records support release or formal escalation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Corrective-action escalation and audit-ready closure
workflow_stages.description: Prepare corrective-action packages, incident escalation notes, and closure support only when the underlying post-trade records, control attestations, and owner assignments support a traceable disposition.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Order blotter records, execution fills, block and sleeve allocations, and average-price instructions
Broker confirms, matched or unmatched statuses, affirmation timestamps, and counterparty workflow messages
Standing settlement instructions, ALERT or tenant SSI golden-source records, custodian-account details, and settlement-location data
Settlement-status records, DK and RAD exceptions, fail-aging logs, cancel and rebook commentary, and break notes
Case-management tickets, control attestations, recurring-incident logs, and approval-history artifacts
Tenant policy maps for market cut-offs, manual-intervention rules, dual controls, and escalation ownership
systems_of_record: Order and execution management systems or trade blotter repositories
Allocation and matching platforms such as CTM or equivalent institutional-trade-processing systems
SSI golden-source repositories such as ALERT or tenant-managed settlement-instruction stores
Custodian, prime-broker, and settlement-status portals or feeds
Post-trade exception, fail, and case-management systems
Document repositories and books-and-records stores supporting audit-ready retrieval
Tenant policy, control, and escalation registries

allowed: Determine whether available post-trade evidence supports hold, release recommendation, investigation, or corrective-action posture within trade-operations control workflows.
Produce evidence-backed trade-control summaries, nonconformance records, and escalation packages tied to named blotter, matching, affirmation, SSI, and exception records.
Request the minimum tenant facts, system hooks, and source-of-truth records needed before continuing safely.
escalate: A product, market, or jurisdiction boundary means the public T+1 baseline may not control the workflow.
A recurring fail, DK, RAD, stale SSI, or unmatched pattern needs formal control, settlements, or compliance ownership.
Public guidance and tenant policy conflict materially on whether the work may proceed.
refuse: Legal interpretation, final compliance signoff, or restricted-list release authority
Settlement release, cash movement approval, or control override without authoritative evidence and owner approval

domain_failure_modes: Treating a matched trade as settlement-ready even though SSI, custodian-account, or settlement-location evidence is missing or contradictory.
Allowing same-day urgency to override affirmation, cut-off, or manual-control requirements without formal authority.
Claiming closure from a spreadsheet or email when authoritative blotter, matching, affirmation, or books-and-records evidence is unavailable.
Ignoring recurring DK, RAD, or fail-aging patterns that should trigger corrective-action escalation rather than one-off commentary.
Overgeneralizing the industry label and applying the same control logic to exempt products, non-U.S. markets, OTC derivatives, or corporate-actions workflows without checking scope.
Collapsing trade-operations control work into settlements authority, compliance interpretation, or portfolio management.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority
Request to bypass a documented safety or quality control
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Product and market scope exceptions to the U.S. T+1 baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.note: Certain government, municipal, foreign, derivative, and other product workflows may follow different timing or matching rules. The package treats missing product and jurisdiction context as an escalation condition rather than guessing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific operating-model splits across asset manager, outsourced middle office, custodian, and prime broker
unresolved_ambiguities.note: Public sources define the baseline records and timing, but the authoritative owner for SSI maintenance, exception approval, and final release varies by tenant and must be retrieved at runtime.
